citation_and_locator: Law 2001, A-not-A Questions in Cantonese, UCL Working Papers in Linguistics 13, examples (60) and (63), article pp. 315-316 / PDF pp. 20-21
what_it_supports: Full overt 係唔係 precedes an overt clause or predicate complement in the cited examples.
limit: Law's uniform copular analysis is contested; the executable Chinese-character strings normalize the source romanization and do not establish every complement class.

citation_and_locator: Li 2017, The Syntactic and Pragmatic Properties of A-not-A Question in Chinese, discussion of Cantonese 係唔係 and tags, PDF pp. 52-55 and 74-75
what_it_supports: Records the analytical dispute and distinguishes tag uses from regular A-not-A questions.
limit: The three-film sample contains no ordinary 係唔係 token and cannot establish absence, rarity, or the runtime's positive complement scope.

citation_and_locator: Wong 2023, Language Sample Analysis in Clinical Linguistics, copula and tag question sections, printed pp. 54-55 / PDF pp. 64-65
what_it_supports: Distinguishes clause-internal copula questions, where 係唔係 or contracted 係咪 precedes the main predicate, from utterance-final tag questions such as 你唔去喇，係唔係呀.
limit: A descriptive clinical coding distinction does not settle the syntax of every complement or require the runtime to merge 係咪 with the full form.

citation_and_locator: Yip 1988, Negation in Cantonese as a Lexical Rule, p. 449 and discussion of suppletive 有/冇
what_it_supports: Supports keeping ordinary 唔-negation in 係唔係 separate from the suppletive 有/冇 family.
limit: Does not establish the complement inventory or tag boundary of 係唔係.
